titleStmt.title: Letter from Arthur Hamilton Norway to Matthew Nathan, 31 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Arthur Hamilton Norway

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Arthur Hamilton Norway (1859–1938), assistant Secretary of the General Post Office, to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862–1939), under-secretary for Ireland, proposing Robert Lonsdale, the Crown Solicitor for Leitrim, for the position of Chief Crown Solicitor. The position had become available on the death of Malachy J. Kelly.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5760/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Put with other
applications
M.N.
1/4/16 31 March 1916 Dear Sir Matthew, I am asked to draw
your attention to the application of
Mr Lonsdale, Crown Solicitor for
Leitrim, for the post of Chief
Crown Solicitor, vacated by the death
of Sir Malachi Kelly. I believe Mr Lonsdale,
who is one of the oldest crown
Solicitors, is recognised as being
a clever capable man, and free
from active political associations. I am,
Yours Sincerely Ce. Ft Norway
